Title: DID-based SME identity registration
Description: Context: SMEs need verifiable digital identity before they can participate in the platform. Business problem: manual identity handling reduces trust and slows onboarding. User value: SMEs and admins can register and verify identity with stronger assurance. Scope: SME identity registration, VC submission, cryptographic verification, identity record creation. Out of scope: credential revocation lifecycle and advanced federation.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R01] Given an SME submits registration details with a valid verifiable credential, when verification is executed, then the platform creates an SME identity record and stores the verification result. | [ReqID: R01] Given an SME submits an invalid or tampered credential, when verification is executed, then registration is rejected and an audit entry is recorded.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for backlog seeding from srs-v3 on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Deferred after MVP.

Title: KYC and AML onboarding workflow
Description: Context: regulated participants require onboarding controls before transacting. Business problem: without KYC and AML workflow the platform cannot safely support regulated financing activity. User value: compliance teams and banks can assess onboarding status consistently. Scope: onboarding intake, KYC review status, AML flagging, audit retention. Out of scope: external sanctions engine procurement.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R02] Given a new SME onboarding case is submitted, when required KYC fields and documents are complete, then the case enters compliance review with a traceable status. | [ReqID: R02] Given an SME fails a KYC or AML rule, when review is completed, then the SME is flagged or blocked from transacting and the decision is logged.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for MVP on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Regulated area; centralized-first workflow acceptable for MVP.

Title: Permissioned membership and role management
Description: Context: the platform requires controlled membership and role assignment for participating organizations and internal operators. Business problem: unmanaged access creates governance and security risk. User value: admins can onboard organizations and enforce least-privilege access. Scope: member registration, role assignment, activation, deactivation, access enforcement. Out of scope: decentralized consortium governance automation.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given an administrator creates a member organization and assigns roles, when the record is saved, then the member can authenticate and access only permitted functions. | [ReqID: R03] Given a member is removed or deactivated, when access is attempted, then protected actions are denied and the attempt is logged.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for MVP on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Foundational MVP capability.

Title: EPCIS-compatible shipment and receipt event recording
Description: Context: supply-chain milestone events need a common exchange format. Business problem: inconsistent event capture reduces interoperability and traceability. User value: buyers and logistics partners can exchange standardized shipment and receipt events. Scope: EPCIS-aligned event ingestion, validation, storage, export. Out of scope: advanced logistics analytics.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R04] Given a shipment or receipt event is submitted in the supported event format, when validation runs, then the event is accepted only if it conforms to the required schema. | [ReqID: R04] Given a valid event exists, when an export is requested, then the platform returns an EPCIS-compatible event payload.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for backlog seeding from srs-v3 on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Deferred after MVP.

Title: Immutable audit trail for procure-to-pay events
Description: Context: procure-to-pay actions require end-to-end traceability. Business problem: fragmented records make audits and dispute investigation difficult. User value: auditors and operators can trace PO, delivery, invoice, and settlement activity reliably. Scope: event capture, timestamping, actor traceability, hash or immutable reference storage. Out of scope: regulator export bundle formatting.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R05] Given a PO, delivery, invoice, or settlement action occurs, when the transaction is committed, then the platform records the event with timestamp, actor identity, and immutable reference data. | [ReqID: R05] Given an auditor opens a transaction history, when the audit trail is queried, then the ordered lifecycle of related events is displayed without gaps.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for MVP on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. MVP may use centralized-first immutable audit storage or hybrid anchoring per C05-C06.

Title: Order acceptance and escrow contract workflow
Description: Context: order acceptance and escrow handling are core transaction controls for financing-enabled procurement. Business problem: manual settlement logic is error-prone and hard to verify. User value: buyers, SMEs, and financiers can execute order and escrow flow with enforceable rules. Scope: order acceptance, escrow state transitions, release conditions, settlement triggers. Out of scope: full multi-party production smart-contract network operations.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R06] Given an approved order with escrow terms exists, when the buyer accepts the order, then the contract workflow creates an escrow state and records the governing terms. | [ReqID: R06] Given the defined proof conditions are satisfied, when release is triggered, then the escrow status changes and the release action is recorded for audit.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for MVP on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. MVP implementation may be workflow-driven or hybrid anchored per C05-C06.

Title: PLS contract model and distribution workflow
Description: Context: the platform must support Shariah-compliant profit and loss sharing products. Business problem: PLS rules require explicit, auditable parameterization and controlled execution. User value: banks and Shariah reviewers can create and track compliant PLS contracts. Scope: contract creation, parameter capture, distribution calculation inputs, distribution record lifecycle. Out of scope: advanced portfolio optimization.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R07] Given a PLS contract is created with approved parameters, when the contract is activated, then the platform stores profit ratio, loss allocation, and linked parties in an auditable record. | [ReqID: R07] Given a distribution event is executed for an active contract, when the calculation is confirmed, then the resulting allocation is recorded against the contract history.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for MVP on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Regulated area; MVP scope limited to essential contract workflow.

Title: Tokenized receivables issuance and redemption
Description: Context: receivables financing requires a traceable digital representation of the underlying invoice. Business problem: without controlled token lifecycle, provenance and redemption checks are weak. User value: financiers can issue, transfer, and redeem receivable representations with traceability. Scope: token issuance, provenance link, transfer, redemption status. Out of scope: public-chain interoperability.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R08] Given an eligible invoice is approved for financing, when token issuance is requested, then the platform creates a receivable token linked to the original invoice and records provenance metadata. | [ReqID: R08] Given a receivable token is redeemed, when redemption is processed, then the token status changes to redeemed and the history shows the full lifecycle.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for MVP on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. MVP scope limited to sandboxable issuance and redemption flow.

Title: Transaction endorsement policy validation
Description: Context: sensitive transactions require policy-based approval before acceptance. Business problem: without endorsement validation, unauthorized transactions may be accepted. User value: admins can enforce transaction acceptance rules consistently. Scope: endorsement policy definition, signature validation, rejection handling. Out of scope: complex multi-network policy orchestration.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R09] Given a transaction requires named endorsers, when the transaction is submitted without all required endorsements, then the platform rejects it with a policy violation result. | [ReqID: R09] Given a transaction includes the required endorsements, when validation runs, then the platform accepts the transaction and stores the endorsement evidence.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for backlog seeding from srs-v3 on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Deferred after MVP.

Title: Privacy channels and private data visibility controls
Description: Context: some commercial terms are sensitive and should only be visible to authorized parties. Business problem: broad data exposure increases privacy and confidentiality risk. User value: permitted participants can view sensitive data while others see only restricted proofs or summaries. Scope: authorization checks, restricted data views, proof or hash visibility for non-permitted users. Out of scope: advanced data masking analytics.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R10] Given a user with authorized access opens a protected record, when the record is loaded, then the user can view the sensitive fields allowed by policy. | [ReqID: R10] Given a user without authorized access requests the same record, when access is evaluated, then the sensitive fields are hidden and only the permitted proof or metadata is returned.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for backlog seeding from srs-v3 on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Deferred after MVP.

Title: ISO20022-compatible payment API gateway
Description: Context: external payment integration requires a standardized messaging interface. Business problem: ad hoc payment mapping increases integration friction and reconciliation defects. User value: banks and integrators can exchange payment requests using a known structure. Scope: payment message mapping, API submission, validation, response handling. Out of scope: production-grade bank certification.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R11] Given a payment request is created from a platform transaction, when the API payload is generated, then the required ISO20022-compatible fields are populated according to the mapping rules. | [ReqID: R11] Given a payload missing a required mapped field is submitted, when validation runs, then the request is rejected with a validation error.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for backlog seeding from srs-v3 on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Deferred after MVP due integration cost C04.

Title: Asynchronous reconciliation event processing
Description: Context: transaction reconciliation may need asynchronous processing. Business problem: synchronous-only reconciliation increases latency and operational fragility. User value: operators can process and retry reconciliation events reliably. Scope: event publishing, retry handling, completion status, operational visibility. Out of scope: enterprise message bus migration.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R12] Given a reconciliation-triggering event is published, when the event consumer processes it successfully, then the reconciliation status is updated within the defined workflow. | [ReqID: R12] Given event processing fails, when retry policy is applied, then the event is retried and the failure history is recorded.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for backlog seeding from srs-v3 on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Deferred after MVP.

Title: Configurable transaction throughput baseline
Description: Context: the platform needs a measurable throughput target to guide delivery and testing. Business problem: without a defined baseline the team cannot validate performance expectations. User value: operators and stakeholders can assess whether the platform meets agreed throughput goals. Scope: configurable throughput target, load test scenario, latency measurement, pass fail criteria. Out of scope: horizontal scale architecture redesign.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R13] Given a configured throughput target exists, when load tests are executed, then the platform reports whether sustained throughput and latency remain within the target thresholds. | [ReqID: R13] Given measured results fall below the accepted baseline, when test execution completes, then a performance failure result is recorded with supporting metrics.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for backlog seeding from srs-v3 on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Deferred after MVP.

Title: Machine-readable procurement metadata publishing
Description: Context: digital procurement transparency requires structured metadata. Business problem: unstructured tender information is harder to reuse, validate, and audit. User value: procurement officers and regulators can publish and consume tender metadata consistently. Scope: metadata capture, validation, machine-readable export. Out of scope: public procurement portal redesign.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R14] Given a tender record is created, when required metadata fields are completed, then the platform stores the tender with the supported machine-readable structure. | [ReqID: R14] Given a valid tender record exists, when metadata export is requested, then the platform returns the required machine-readable metadata fields.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for backlog seeding from srs-v3 on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Deferred after MVP.

Title: Regulator audit reporting and signed export bundle
Description: Context: regulators and auditors need a consumable audit package from platform records. Business problem: raw event data alone is difficult to review and transmit. User value: regulators can request and receive signed audit exports within an agreed turnaround. Scope: audit bundle generation, signing, export request handling, delivery status tracking. Out of scope: regulator portal integration.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R15] Given an authorized regulator export request is submitted, when the export job completes, then the platform generates a signed audit bundle containing the requested records. | [ReqID: R15] Given an export bundle has been generated, when the requester downloads it, then the bundle includes integrity metadata sufficient to verify authenticity.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for MVP on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. MVP scope excludes external regulator integration.

Title: Upgradeable contract versioning workflow
Description: Context: contract logic and templates will evolve over time. Business problem: unmanaged upgrades create compatibility and audit risk. User value: developers and reviewers can upgrade contract versions with traceability. Scope: version tagging, upgrade path, migration support, audit history. Out of scope: automated multi-network migration.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R16] Given a new contract version is prepared, when the upgrade process is executed, then the platform records the prior version, new version, and upgrade metadata. | [ReqID: R16] Given an upgrade affects existing records, when migration validation is run, then the platform reports whether affected records remain accessible or are migrated successfully.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for backlog seeding from srs-v3 on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Deferred after MVP.

Title: Role-based UI and operational dashboards
Description: Context: different actors require different workflows and visibility. Business problem: a one-size-fits-all interface increases error rate and reduces productivity. User value: each role can view relevant data and actions only. Scope: role-based navigation, dashboard widgets, role action permissions, tailored views. Out of scope: advanced analytics personalization.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R17] Given a user logs in with a defined platform role, when the dashboard loads, then the user sees the role-specific widgets and actions assigned to that role. | [ReqID: R17] Given a user attempts to access an action outside the assigned role, when authorization is evaluated, then the action is hidden or blocked.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for MVP on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. MVP should focus on essential role views only.

Title: Delivery evidence with signed IoT or QR proof
Description: Context: proof of delivery supports settlement and dispute reduction. Business problem: delivery confirmation without verifiable evidence is weaker for financing and audit. User value: buyers and logistics parties can verify delivery evidence linked to transactions. Scope: signed proof capture, linkage to delivery event, verification result display. Out of scope: hardware device management.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R18] Given a delivery event has a signed IoT or QR proof, when the proof is submitted, then the platform verifies the signature and links the proof to the delivery record. | [ReqID: R18] Given a linked proof exists, when a buyer reviews the delivery record, then the proof status and verification result are visible.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for backlog seeding from srs-v3 on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Deferred after MVP.

Title: Data retention and legal-hold controls
Description: Context: transaction and compliance records must follow retention rules. Business problem: uncontrolled deletion or expiry can violate legal obligations. User value: auditors and legal users can enforce retention and legal-hold states. Scope: retention policy application, hold flagging, deletion prevention during hold. Out of scope: cross-jurisdiction rules engine.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R19] Given a record reaches a retention checkpoint, when the retention policy is evaluated, then the platform applies the configured retention state to the record. | [ReqID: R19] Given a legal hold is active on a record, when deletion or purge is attempted, then the action is blocked and logged.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for backlog seeding from srs-v3 on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Deferred after MVP.

Title: Shariah governance workflow for PLS approvals
Description: Context: PLS products require governance review before use. Business problem: without a formal approval workflow, compliant activation cannot be demonstrated. User value: Shariah reviewers and banks can track review outcomes before product activation. Scope: checklist workflow, review decision, approval history, activation gate. Out of scope: external board portal.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given a new PLS product or contract type is submitted for review, when the checklist is completed and approved, then the platform records the approval result and allows activation. | [ReqID: R20] Given required governance approval is missing, when activation is attempted, then the platform blocks activation and shows the missing approval state.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for MVP on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Regulated area; MVP requires explicit audit history.

Title: Dispute resolution and arbitration record workflow
Description: Context: procurement and financing flows may require formal dispute handling. Business problem: inconsistent dispute tracking weakens governance and evidence management. User value: parties can record disputes, evidence, and final status in one lifecycle. Scope: dispute creation, evidence attachment metadata, status updates, final resolution record. Out of scope: external arbitration system integration.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R21] Given a transaction dispute is raised, when the case is created, then the platform stores the dispute record with linked transaction context and initial status. | [ReqID: R21] Given a dispute reaches resolution, when the final decision is recorded, then the platform stores the final status and linked evidence history.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for backlog seeding from srs-v3 on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Deferred after MVP.

Title: Access logging and cryptographic non-repudiation
Description: Context: regulated and sensitive workflows require strong auditability of user actions. Business problem: incomplete access logging undermines investigations and compliance evidence. User value: auditors and security operators can trace who did what and prove action origin. Scope: access logs, signed action records, query support for investigation. Out of scope: external SIEM integration.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R22] Given a user accesses or changes a protected resource, when the action is processed, then the platform records the action with actor identity, timestamp, and cryptographic or equivalent non-repudiation evidence. | [ReqID: R22] Given an auditor reviews access history, when a search is executed, then the platform returns a traceable sequence of logged actions for the selected user or resource.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for MVP on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Foundational MVP control.

Title: Legacy ERP interoperability adapter
Description: Context: enterprise buyers and suppliers may need existing ERP systems to exchange procurement data with the platform. Business problem: manual transfer reduces adoption and increases errors. User value: integrators can map PO and invoice flows between systems. Scope: adapter mappings, sample import export, error handling. Out of scope: full production ERP rollout.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R23] Given a sample PO or invoice payload is received from a supported legacy format, when mapping is executed, then the adapter produces the platform-compatible payload. | [ReqID: R23] Given a mapped payload is missing required fields, when validation runs, then the adapter rejects the payload and reports the mapping error.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for backlog seeding from srs-v3 on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Deferred after MVP due C04.

Title: Consent management and privacy controls
Description: Context: user and organization data sharing requires explicit consent handling. Business problem: exports and sharing without tracked consent create privacy risk. User value: SMEs can grant and revoke consent and see that exports honor the current consent state. Scope: consent grant, consent revoke, consent check on export, audit history. Out of scope: advanced preference center.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R24] Given an SME grants consent for a defined data use, when the consent is saved, then the platform records the consent state with scope and timestamp. | [ReqID: R24] Given consent has been revoked, when an export requiring that consent is requested, then the platform blocks or limits the export according to the current consent state.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for backlog seeding from srs-v3 on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Deferred after MVP.

Title: Monitoring dashboards and incident management hooks
Description: Context: platform operations require visibility and response readiness. Business problem: poor operational telemetry increases recovery time and hidden failures. User value: operators can monitor health, detect incidents, and follow response triggers. Scope: dashboards, alert conditions, incident hook integration, basic runbook linkage. Out of scope: full NOC implementation.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R25] Given a monitored service experiences a defined failure condition, when the threshold is crossed, then the platform triggers an alert and records the incident event. | [ReqID: R25] Given an operator opens the monitoring dashboard, when the page loads, then current service health and recent alerts are visible.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for backlog seeding from srs-v3 on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Deferred after MVP.

Title: Reproducible PLS scenario test harness
Description: Context: PLS calculations and workflows require repeatable verification. Business problem: without a repeatable harness the team cannot validate business scenarios consistently. User value: QA and developers can rerun known profit and loss scenarios with expected outputs. Scope: scenario definitions, repeatable execution, expected result comparison, test evidence. Out of scope: enterprise-scale performance suite.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R26] Given a defined PLS scenario exists in the harness, when the scenario is executed, then the platform produces the expected profit and loss result for comparison. | [ReqID: R26] Given the scenario output differs from the expected result, when the test run completes, then the harness marks the scenario as failed and stores the evidence.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for backlog seeding from srs-v3 on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Deferred after MVP.

Title: Identity and credential revocation
Description: Context: issued credentials may later become invalid or withdrawn. Business problem: without revocation support invalid credentials may continue to pass verification. User value: admins can revoke a credential and immediately affect verification outcomes. Scope: revoke action, revocation status lookup, verification check. Out of scope: cross-network revocation federation.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R27] Given an authorized admin revokes a credential, when revocation is confirmed, then the credential status changes to revoked in the verification store. | [ReqID: R27] Given a revoked credential is presented for verification, when the verification check runs, then the platform returns an invalid result and blocks the protected action.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for backlog seeding from srs-v3 on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Deferred after MVP.

Title: Regulatory sandbox mode and audit hooks
Description: Context: pilot deployments may require a restricted sandbox operating mode. Business problem: without a sandbox mode, regulator-facing pilots may be harder to isolate and audit. User value: teams can run a restricted pilot mode with enhanced logging. Scope: sandbox mode flag, restricted deployment behavior, enhanced audit hooks. Out of scope: production regulator portal.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R28] Given sandbox mode is enabled for an environment, when users operate within that environment, then the platform applies the defined sandbox restrictions and enhanced logging rules. | [ReqID: R28] Given sandbox mode is disabled, when standard operations run, then sandbox-only restrictions are not applied.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for backlog seeding from srs-v3 on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Deferred after MVP.

Title: Multi-jurisdiction retention and consent configuration
Description: Context: different operating jurisdictions may require different retention and consent rules. Business problem: hard-coded policy behavior does not scale across jurisdictions. User value: admins can apply jurisdiction-specific privacy and retention settings. Scope: configurable rules by jurisdiction, policy lookup, testable rule application. Out of scope: legal interpretation engine.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R29] Given a record is associated with a configured jurisdiction, when retention or consent policy is evaluated, then the platform applies the rules defined for that jurisdiction. | [ReqID: R29] Given a jurisdiction configuration is changed, when policy tests are executed, then the platform reflects the new rules in the test results.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for backlog seeding from srs-v3 on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Deferred after MVP.

Title: Consortium governance policy registry
Description: Context: multi-party platforms require explicit governance records for policy changes. Business problem: undocumented governance decisions weaken accountability and trust. User value: consortium admins can record bylaws and policy changes with traceable approval outcomes. Scope: policy registry, change proposal, approval record, history view. Out of scope: automated quorum execution across independent organizations.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R30] Given a governance policy change is proposed, when the proposal is submitted, then the platform stores the policy record with proposer, date, and current status. | [ReqID: R30] Given a policy change is approved, when the decision is finalized, then the registry records the approval outcome and preserves the policy history.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for backlog seeding from srs-v3 on 2026-03-10. Constraint refs: C01-C07. Deferred after MVP.

Title: As an administrator, I want to register a member organization, so that participating organizations can be onboarded into the platform
Description: Context: the platform requires a controlled member registry before roles and access can be managed. Business problem: unmanaged organization onboarding creates governance and security gaps. User role / actor: administrator. Expected behavior: administrator creates a member organization record with essential membership metadata and initial status. Business value: establishes the baseline organization registry for the platform. In scope: organization creation, uniqueness validation, membership status initialization. Out of scope: role assignment and protected-function enforcement.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given an administrator submits valid organization details, when the organization is created, then the platform stores the member organization with a unique identifier and active status. | [ReqID: R03] Given required organization data is missing or duplicated, when creation is attempted, then the platform blocks the request and shows validation errors.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for Sprint 1 decomposition of PBI-003.

Title: As an administrator, I want to define and maintain platform roles, so that access can be governed consistently
Description: Context: role-based access requires a stable role catalogue before user assignment. Business problem: inconsistent role definitions weaken access governance. User role / actor: administrator. Expected behavior: administrator can create, update, and manage platform roles used for membership access control. Business value: establishes a reusable role model for secure access management. In scope: role creation, role update, role status management. Out of scope: user-role assignment and runtime access denial.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given an administrator defines a valid role, when the role is saved, then the platform stores the role for future assignment. | [ReqID: R03] Given a duplicate or invalid role definition is submitted, when save is attempted, then the platform rejects the change and returns a validation error.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for Sprint 1 decomposition of PBI-003.

Title: As an administrator, I want to assign platform roles to member users, so that each user can access only the functions required for their responsibilities
Description: Context: once organizations and roles exist, users must be bound to those roles to support controlled access. Business problem: users without structured role assignment cannot be governed consistently. User role / actor: administrator. Expected behavior: administrator assigns, changes, or removes roles for users associated with member organizations. Business value: enables least-privilege access control. In scope: assign role, change role, remove role, membership validation. Out of scope: deactivation enforcement.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given a user belongs to a valid member organization and a valid role exists, when an administrator assigns the role, then the platform stores the assignment successfully. | [ReqID: R03] Given the user is not associated with a valid member organization or the role is invalid, when assignment is attempted, then the platform blocks the assignment and shows the reason.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for Sprint 1 decomposition of PBI-003.

Title: As the platform, I want to block access for deactivated members, so that removed organizations and users cannot use protected functions
Description: Context: membership and role management are incomplete without enforcement against deactivated members. Business problem: inactive members retaining access creates governance and security risk. User role / actor: system enforcement. Expected behavior: protected actions are denied when a member organization or user is deactivated. Business value: ensures access control reflects membership state. In scope: deactivation state check, denial of protected actions, error feedback. Out of scope: advanced non-repudiation analytics.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given a member organization or user is deactivated, when a protected action is attempted, then the platform denies access. | [ReqID: R03] Given an active member with a valid role attempts a protected action, when access is evaluated, then the platform allows the action to proceed.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for Sprint 1 decomposition of PBI-003.

Title: As a compliance coordinator, I want to submit a PLS item for Shariah review, so that governance starts from a formal and traceable request
Description: Context: Shariah governance requires a formal starting point for review. Business problem: ad hoc review initiation makes approval workflow ambiguous and difficult to track. User role / actor: compliance coordinator. Expected behavior: coordinator submits a review request with the mandatory metadata for a PLS item. Business value: creates a controlled and traceable approval intake process. In scope: request creation, mandatory fields, initial workflow state. Out of scope: checklist completion and final decision.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given the required review-request data is complete, when the coordinator submits the request, then the platform creates a review record with initial Submitted status. | [ReqID: R20] Given mandatory review-request data is incomplete, when submission is attempted, then the platform blocks the request and shows validation errors.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for Sprint 1 decomposition of PBI-020.

Title: As a Shariah reviewer, I want to record the review checklist outcome, so that the basis of the governance decision is documented
Description: Context: governance decisions require structured review evidence before approval can be finalized. Business problem: undocumented checklist completion weakens governance assurance. User role / actor: Shariah reviewer. Expected behavior: reviewer completes and saves the checklist findings for a submitted review item. Business value: makes the approval basis explicit and auditable. In scope: checklist completion, reviewer comments, review completion state. Out of scope: final approve reject conditional-approve transition.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given a submitted review item exists, when the reviewer completes the checklist with all required entries, then the platform stores the checklist outcome and reviewer comments. | [ReqID: R20] Given mandatory checklist entries are missing, when the reviewer attempts to save completion, then the platform blocks completion and shows validation errors.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for Sprint 1 decomposition of PBI-020.

Title: As a Shariah reviewer, I want to record approve, reject, or conditional-approve decisions, so that governance outcomes are explicit and auditable
Description: Context: the review workflow must end in a formal governance outcome. Business problem: without explicit decision capture, approval status cannot be relied upon. User role / actor: Shariah reviewer. Expected behavior: reviewer records the final decision with rationale or conditions where applicable. Business value: enables clear governance outcomes and later compliance verification. In scope: approve, reject, conditional approve, rationale capture, state transition. Out of scope: activation gating against PLS contract execution.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given a completed review checklist exists, when the reviewer records an approval decision, then the platform changes the item status to Approved and stores the decision metadata. | [ReqID: R20] Given the reviewer records a rejection or conditional approval, when the decision is saved, then the platform stores the rationale or conditions and updates the workflow state accordingly.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for Sprint 1 decomposition of PBI-020.

Title: As a compliance coordinator, I want to view the current approval status and decision history, so that I can track governance progress and outcomes
Description: Context: governance workflow participants need visibility into approval progress and results. Business problem: without a consolidated view, coordinators cannot follow up or confirm outcome state efficiently. User role / actor: compliance coordinator. Expected behavior: coordinator can view the latest status and prior review history of a PLS review item. Business value: improves workflow transparency and operational follow-up. In scope: status view, decision history, summary of review progression. Out of scope: regulator export and linkage to downstream contract execution.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given a review item exists, when the coordinator opens its details, then the platform displays the current approval status and prior review history. | [ReqID: R20] Given no final decision has been recorded yet, when the coordinator views the item, then the platform still shows the submitted and review-progress states without error.
Notes: DoR: Ready. PO Approval: Approved for Sprint 1 decomposition of PBI-020.

Title: Do define member organization schema, uniqueness rules, and membership status model
Description: Action to be performed: define the member organization data model, required fields, uniqueness rules, and active or inactive lifecycle states. Why it is needed: downstream API, UI, and access-control tasks depend on stable data and state rules. Expected output: reviewed schema, validation rules, and request-response contract. Scope: entity fields, uniqueness rule, state values, API contract. Explicit exclusions: UI build and endpoint implementation.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given the schema draft is reviewed, when validation rules are checked, then required fields, uniqueness constraints, and status values are explicitly defined. | [ReqID: R03] Given downstream tasks consume the model, when implementation starts, then the request and response contract is stable enough for API and UI work.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-031 AC1 and AC2. Shared prerequisite for PBI-040 and PBI-041. Evidence: schema note, validation matrix, API contract.

Title: Do implement member organization creation API and service validation
Description: Action to be performed: build the backend endpoint and service logic for member organization creation with required validation and duplicate checks. Why it is needed: the story requires creation of unique active member organizations. Expected output: working create service with persistence and validation behavior. Scope: endpoint, service logic, required-field validation, duplicate prevention, persistence. Explicit exclusions: admin screen implementation.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given valid organization input is submitted, when the create service executes, then the organization is stored with a unique identifier and active status. | [ReqID: R03] Given missing or duplicate data is submitted, when validation runs, then the service rejects the request with the correct error response.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-031 AC1 and AC2. True prerequisite for final verification. Evidence: API tests, negative tests, sample payloads.

Title: Do implement administrator registration UI and error feedback for member onboarding
Description: Action to be performed: build the admin form and user feedback for member organization registration. Why it is needed: administrators need a usable interface to create organizations and handle invalid input. Expected output: working form with validation messages and success state. Scope: form fields, submit flow, validation feedback, success and error states. Explicit exclusions: backend authorization and audit logging.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given valid registration data is entered, when the administrator submits the form, then the UI sends the request and displays a success result. | [ReqID: R03] Given invalid or duplicate data is entered, when submission is attempted, then the UI displays meaningful validation or error feedback.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-031 AC1 and AC2. Can run in parallel with PBI-040 after contract is stable. Evidence: screenshots, functional checks.

Title: Do implement organization-creation authorization, audit logging, and protected data handling
Description: Action to be performed: add authorization checks, audit events, and sensitive-field handling to organization creation. Why it is needed: the workflow is governance-sensitive and must be restricted and traceable. Expected output: admin-only creation path with audit records. Scope: permission checks, create-event logging, protected-field rules. Explicit exclusions: advanced reporting.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given a non-admin attempts organization creation, when access is evaluated, then the operation is denied. | [ReqID: R03] Given an authorized organization creation succeeds, when the action completes, then an audit record is written with actor and timestamp.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-031 AC1. Supports DoD compliance and traceability. Evidence: access-control test results, log samples.

Title: Do execute end-to-end validation, regression checks, documentation updates, and demo evidence for member organization registration
Description: Action to be performed: validate the full member-organization registration flow, update documentation, and collect evidence. Why it is needed: the story cannot meet Done without proof, regression safety, and supporting documentation. Expected output: completed test evidence, docs updates, and demo-ready flow. Scope: functional testing, regression checks, docs, demo evidence. Explicit exclusions: new feature development.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given the implementation tasks are complete, when end-to-end validation is run, then both valid and invalid registration paths pass. | [ReqID: R03] Given the story is prepared for review, when evidence is collected, then test results, screenshots, and updated documentation are linked.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-031 AC1 and AC2 plus DoD. Evidence task; must exist before PO acceptance.

Title: Do define role catalog model, constraints, and reference data
Description: Action to be performed: define the platform role model, allowed attributes, and role constraints. Why it is needed: role assignment and access behavior depend on a stable role catalog. Expected output: reviewed role model and validation rules. Scope: role entity, uniqueness, role status, reference data. Explicit exclusions: assignment workflow.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given the role model is reviewed, when constraints are checked, then allowed role attributes and uniqueness rules are explicitly defined. | [ReqID: R03] Given downstream implementation depends on the model, when build starts, then the role contract is stable enough for API and UI tasks.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-032 AC1 and AC2. Shared prerequisite for PBI-045 and PBI-046. Evidence: role model, validation rules.

Title: Do implement role management API and duplicate or invalid-role validation
Description: Action to be performed: build the backend role create and update logic with validation. Why it is needed: the role catalog must be maintainable and protected from invalid definitions. Expected output: working role-management service with validation. Scope: create and update service, duplicate prevention, invalid-role checks, persistence. Explicit exclusions: role UI.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given a valid role definition is submitted, when the service runs, then the role is stored or updated successfully. | [ReqID: R03] Given a duplicate or invalid definition is submitted, when validation runs, then the request is rejected with the correct error.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-032 AC1 and AC2. Downstream prerequisite for role assignment story. Evidence: API tests, negative cases.

Title: Do implement administrator role management UI and system feedback
Description: Action to be performed: build the administrator interface for creating and maintaining roles with clear feedback. Why it is needed: role maintenance must be usable and testable in Sprint 1. Expected output: working role-management screen and feedback behavior. Scope: role form, update screen, validation messages, status display. Explicit exclusions: authorization middleware.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given valid role data is entered, when the administrator saves it, then the UI shows success and the role is available for later assignment. | [ReqID: R03] Given invalid or duplicate role data is entered, when save is attempted, then the UI shows the appropriate validation or error message.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-032 AC1 and AC2. Can proceed in parallel with PBI-045 after contract is fixed. Evidence: screenshots, UI tests.

Title: Do implement authorization boundaries, audit logging, and role status checks
Description: Action to be performed: add permission checks, audit events, and active-inactive role handling to role management. Why it is needed: governance requires controlled and traceable role changes. Expected output: protected role-management actions and audit records. Scope: admin-only role maintenance, audit logging, inactive-role rules. Explicit exclusions: analytics.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given a non-admin attempts to create or update a role, when access is evaluated, then the operation is denied. | [ReqID: R03] Given an authorized role change is made, when the action completes, then an audit event is recorded and role status rules are enforced.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-032 AC1. Supports compliance and later role-assignment integrity. Evidence: access tests, log samples.

Title: Do execute role-management testing, regression checks, documentation updates, and demo evidence
Description: Action to be performed: validate role management end to end and prepare evidence and documentation. Why it is needed: story Done requires test proof, safe regression posture, and reviewable artifacts. Expected output: completed tests, updated docs, and demo material. Scope: functional tests, regression checks, docs, demo evidence. Explicit exclusions: new feature implementation.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given implementation tasks are complete, when end-to-end validation is run, then valid and invalid role-management paths pass. | [ReqID: R03] Given the story is reviewed, when evidence is attached, then test results, screenshots, and documentation updates are available.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-032 AC1 and AC2 plus DoD. Needed before PO acceptance.

Title: Do define user-to-organization and user-to-role assignment rules and preconditions
Description: Action to be performed: define the assignment model, allowed preconditions, and invalid scenarios for assigning roles to users. Why it is needed: role assignment depends on organization membership, user availability, and valid roles. Expected output: reviewed assignment rules and failure cases. Scope: preconditions, allowed transitions, duplicate rules, invalid scenarios. Explicit exclusions: endpoint implementation.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given the assignment rules are reviewed, when prerequisites are checked, then valid organization, user, and role preconditions are explicitly defined. | [ReqID: R03] Given invalid scenarios are analyzed, when the rule set is completed, then blocked cases and user feedback requirements are documented.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-033 AC1 and AC2. Assumption: user identity entity already exists or is mockable for Sprint 1.

Title: Do implement role assignment service for assign, change, and remove actions
Description: Action to be performed: build backend logic for assigning, changing, and removing roles from member users. Why it is needed: the story requires executable role-to-user binding with rule enforcement. Expected output: working assignment service and validation behavior. Scope: assign, change, remove, membership validation, invalid-role handling. Explicit exclusions: admin screen.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given a valid user, organization, and role are supplied, when assignment is executed, then the system stores the assignment successfully. | [ReqID: R03] Given the user is invalid, not a member, or the role is invalid, when assignment is attempted, then the request is rejected with the correct error.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-033 AC1 and AC2. Downstream prerequisite for deactivation enforcement. Evidence: API tests, negative path tests.

Title: Do implement administrator role-assignment UI and operational feedback
Description: Action to be performed: build the administrator interface for assigning, changing, and removing user roles with clear feedback. Why it is needed: administrators need a usable workflow for controlled assignment. Expected output: working role-assignment screen with validation messages. Scope: assign-change-remove UI, feedback, member and role lookup. Explicit exclusions: authorization middleware.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given valid assignment inputs are entered, when the administrator submits the change, then the UI shows success and updated assignment state. | [ReqID: R03] Given invalid assignment inputs are entered, when submission is attempted, then the UI displays the reason the action is blocked.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-033 AC1 and AC2. Requires stable API contract from PBI-050. Evidence: screenshots, functional test cases.

Title: Do implement permission checks, audit logging, and invalid-assignment prevention
Description: Action to be performed: add access control, audit trails, and preventive checks for invalid role assignments. Why it is needed: assignment is governance-sensitive and must be restricted and traceable. Expected output: admin-only assignment actions with audit records and blocked invalid operations. Scope: permission checks, audit logs, invalid-assignment prevention. Explicit exclusions: reporting.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given an unauthorized user attempts to assign a role, when access is evaluated, then the action is denied. | [ReqID: R03] Given an invalid or duplicate assignment is attempted, when the system validates it, then the action is blocked and logged as appropriate.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-033 AC1 and AC2. Supports compliance evidence and regression safety. Evidence: log samples, negative tests.

Title: Do execute role-assignment testing with seeded data, documentation updates, and demo evidence
Description: Action to be performed: validate role assignment with realistic data and prepare documentation and evidence. Why it is needed: story Done depends on proof across happy and invalid paths. Expected output: seeded test dataset, results, docs, and demo-ready assignment flow. Scope: seeded data, functional tests, regression tests, docs, demo evidence. Explicit exclusions: new feature logic.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given seeded users, roles, and organizations exist, when role-assignment validation is executed, then valid and invalid scenarios pass as expected. | [ReqID: R03] Given the story is reviewed, when evidence is attached, then test results, screenshots, and updated documentation are available.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-033 AC1 and AC2 plus DoD. Requires test data setup visible in evidence.

Title: Do define deactivation rules, protected-function list, and access-denial behavior
Description: Action to be performed: define what deactivation means for member organizations and users, identify protected functions, and specify denial behavior. Why it is needed: access-blocking implementation fails without explicit rules and target surface. Expected output: reviewed deactivation policy and protected-function inventory. Scope: state rules, protected functions, denial messages, lifecycle effects. Explicit exclusions: code integration.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given deactivation scenarios are reviewed, when the rules are finalized, then the system behavior for active and inactive states is explicitly defined. | [ReqID: R03] Given protected functions are identified, when implementation starts, then the guarded API and UI targets are known.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-034 AC1 and AC2. Hidden assumption to surface early: protected-function list must be agreed before build.

Title: Do implement member and user deactivation handling in access-control services
Description: Action to be performed: add deactivation-aware checks to backend access-control services. Why it is needed: inactive members must be prevented from using protected functions. Expected output: service logic that evaluates active or inactive state before allowing access. Scope: deactivation status checks, effective-access evaluation, service hooks. Explicit exclusions: UI guard implementation.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given a deactivated member or user attempts a protected action, when access is evaluated, then the service denies the action. | [ReqID: R03] Given an active member with valid access attempts a protected action, when access is evaluated, then the service permits the action to continue.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-034 AC1 and AC2. Depends on assignment service realism from PBI-050.

Title: Do integrate protected endpoints and UI guards with deactivation checks
Description: Action to be performed: wire deactivation checks into protected endpoints and user-interface guards with clear feedback. Why it is needed: enforcement must exist at actual interaction points, not only in isolated services. Expected output: guarded API routes and UI actions with denial messages. Scope: endpoint protection, UI guards, error feedback. Explicit exclusions: audit analytics.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given a deactivated member reaches a protected endpoint or action, when the request is made, then the system blocks it and displays the appropriate feedback. | [ReqID: R03] Given an active member uses the same protected function, when access is checked, then the action is allowed.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-034 AC1 and AC2. Integration work is often underestimated; keep endpoint/UI parity visible.

Title: Do implement deactivation audit logging, regression safeguards for active members, and configuration controls
Description: Action to be performed: add logging and safeguards around deactivation enforcement and any needed configuration control. Why it is needed: governance enforcement must be traceable and must not break valid active-user flows. Expected output: audit logs, regression coverage, and configuration notes. Scope: denial logs, active-member regression checks, configuration or feature toggle if needed. Explicit exclusions: reporting dashboards.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given a deactivated-access attempt occurs, when the action is denied, then an audit record is written. | [ReqID: R03] Given active-member scenarios are re-tested, when regression checks run, then valid active-user flows still pass.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-034 AC1 and AC2. Evidence should include both deny and allow logs.

Title: Do execute access-control validation, documentation updates, and demo evidence for deactivated-member blocking
Description: Action to be performed: validate access denial and allow paths, update docs, and collect demo evidence. Why it is needed: the story is not Done without demonstrable behavior and supporting artifacts. Expected output: completed validation and review package. Scope: end-to-end tests, regression checks, docs, demo evidence. Explicit exclusions: new logic development.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R03] Given implementation tasks are complete, when access-control validation is executed, then deactivated members are blocked and active members continue to work. | [ReqID: R03] Given the story is reviewed, when evidence is attached, then test results, screenshots, and log excerpts are available.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-034 AC1 and AC2 plus DoD. Demo should show both blocked and allowed flows.

Title: Do define review-request schema, mandatory metadata, and initial workflow status rules
Description: Action to be performed: define the data contract and initial state rules for Shariah review submissions. Why it is needed: submission, checklist, and later decision stories depend on a stable request structure. Expected output: reviewed review-request schema and Submitted-state rule. Scope: fields, required metadata, state rules, request-response contract. Explicit exclusions: UI and service implementation.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given the request model is reviewed, when the schema is finalized, then mandatory metadata and initial status are explicitly defined. | [ReqID: R20] Given downstream stories consume the request model, when implementation begins, then the contract is stable enough for API and UI work.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-035 AC1 and AC2. Shared prerequisite for PBI-060, PBI-061, and PBI-064.

Title: Do implement review-request API and service validation for submission
Description: Action to be performed: build backend submission logic for Shariah review requests with mandatory validation and Submitted-state transition. Why it is needed: the review workflow must start from a controlled service path. Expected output: working submission API and persistence behavior. Scope: endpoint, required-field validation, persistence, Submitted transition. Explicit exclusions: UI form.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given valid review-request data is submitted, when the service processes it, then the review item is stored with Submitted status. | [ReqID: R20] Given mandatory data is missing, when the service validates the request, then the submission is rejected with the correct error.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-035 AC1 and AC2. Prerequisite for checklist story. Evidence: API tests and state-transition checks.

Title: Do implement coordinator submission UI and validation feedback for review requests
Description: Action to be performed: build the coordinator UI for submitting review items with clear field validation and success feedback. Why it is needed: compliance users need a usable workflow to start review. Expected output: working submission form and feedback behavior. Scope: form, validation feedback, success state, reference capture. Explicit exclusions: permission middleware.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given valid submission data is entered, when the coordinator submits the form, then the UI shows successful creation of the review item. | [ReqID: R20] Given mandatory data is missing, when submission is attempted, then the UI displays validation feedback.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-035 AC1 and AC2. Can proceed in parallel with PBI-060 after contract is fixed.

Title: Do implement request access control, audit logging, and reference or attachment handling
Description: Action to be performed: add permission checks, audit logging, and handling for linked references or attachments on review submission. Why it is needed: governance requests are regulated records and must be traceable and access-controlled. Expected output: protected submission path with logs and managed references. Scope: coordinator permissions, create-event logging, reference handling. Explicit exclusions: external storage optimization.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given an unauthorized user attempts to submit a review request, when access is evaluated, then the request is denied. | [ReqID: R20] Given an authorized request is submitted, when it completes, then an audit record and linked reference metadata are stored.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-035 AC1. Supports evidence and regulated-data handling.

Title: Do execute review-submission validation, documentation updates, and demo evidence
Description: Action to be performed: validate review submission end to end, update supporting docs, and collect evidence. Why it is needed: the story is not Done without proof and review artifacts. Expected output: test results, docs, and demo-ready submission flow. Scope: functional tests, regression checks, docs, screenshots, demo evidence. Explicit exclusions: new business logic.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given implementation tasks are complete, when end-to-end submission validation is executed, then valid and invalid request paths pass. | [ReqID: R20] Given the story is prepared for review, when evidence is attached, then test results, screenshots, and updated documentation are available.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-035 AC1 and AC2 plus DoD. Needed before story closure.

Title: Do define checklist structure, mandatory entries, and completion rules
Description: Action to be performed: define the Shariah review checklist model, required entries, and completion criteria. Why it is needed: checklist persistence, UI, and decisioning depend on stable review evidence rules. Expected output: reviewed checklist schema and completion logic. Scope: checklist items, required entries, completion rules, state implications. Explicit exclusions: final decision rules.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given the checklist model is reviewed, when completion rules are checked, then mandatory entries and completion criteria are explicitly defined. | [ReqID: R20] Given downstream decision work depends on checklist completion, when the model is finalized, then the completion-state behavior is clear enough to implement.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-036 AC1 and AC2. Shared prerequisite for PBI-065 and PBI-069.

Title: Do implement reviewer checklist API and persistence of checklist outcomes
Description: Action to be performed: build backend logic for reviewers to save checklist completion results and comments. Why it is needed: the checklist outcome must be stored and validated before decisioning. Expected output: working checklist service with persistence and validation. Scope: create-update checklist outcome, required-entry validation, persistence. Explicit exclusions: decision outcome service.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given a submitted review item exists and required checklist entries are complete, when the reviewer saves the checklist, then the outcome and comments are stored. | [ReqID: R20] Given required checklist entries are missing, when the reviewer attempts to save completion, then the system rejects the action with validation errors.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-036 AC1 and AC2. True prerequisite for decision story execution.

Title: Do implement reviewer checklist UI and incomplete-entry feedback
Description: Action to be performed: build the reviewer screen for checklist completion with validation feedback. Why it is needed: reviewers need a usable workflow to record checklist outcomes and handle missing entries. Expected output: working checklist screen and feedback behavior. Scope: checklist UI, comment fields, error messages, save flow. Explicit exclusions: final decision form.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given all required checklist entries are completed, when the reviewer saves the checklist, then the UI shows successful completion. | [ReqID: R20] Given mandatory entries are missing, when save is attempted, then the UI highlights the missing items and blocks completion.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-036 AC1 and AC2. Depends on stable checklist contract.

Title: Do implement checklist access control, audit logging, and state-transition guards
Description: Action to be performed: add permissions, audit logging, and valid-state checks to checklist completion. Why it is needed: only the right reviewer in the right workflow state should complete the checklist. Expected output: protected and traceable checklist flow. Scope: reviewer permissions, audit logs, Submitted-to-reviewed guard rules. Explicit exclusions: dashboard reporting.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given an unauthorized user attempts checklist completion, when access is evaluated, then the action is denied. | [ReqID: R20] Given a review item is in the wrong state, when checklist completion is attempted, then the system blocks the transition and records the result appropriately.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-036 AC1 and AC2. Evidence should show both authorization and invalid-state handling.

Title: Do execute checklist validation, regression checks, documentation updates, and demo evidence
Description: Action to be performed: validate checklist completion end to end, update guidance, and gather review evidence. Why it is needed: the story must prove checklist behavior before decisioning can be trusted. Expected output: validated checklist flow and updated docs. Scope: functional tests, regression tests, docs, screenshots, demo evidence. Explicit exclusions: new feature logic.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given implementation tasks are complete, when checklist validation runs, then valid and invalid completion paths pass. | [ReqID: R20] Given the story is prepared for acceptance, when evidence is attached, then tests, screenshots, and updated documentation are available.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-036 AC1 and AC2 plus DoD. Downstream consumer is PBI-037.

Title: Do define decision states, rationale rules, and conditional-approval constraints
Description: Action to be performed: define the formal governance decision model including approval, rejection, and conditional approval rules. Why it is needed: decision logic is high-risk and must be explicit before service and UI implementation. Expected output: reviewed state-transition matrix and field rules. Scope: decision states, mandatory rationale, conditional fields, invalid transitions. Explicit exclusions: API and UI implementation.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given decision outcomes are reviewed, when the decision model is finalized, then valid transitions and mandatory fields are explicitly defined. | [ReqID: R20] Given conditional approval is supported, when the rule set is checked, then condition-specific constraints are documented.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-037 AC1 and AC2. High-risk rules task; decompose early to prevent rework.

Title: Do implement decision API and service logic for approve, reject, and conditional approve
Description: Action to be performed: build backend decision logic for reviewers to record governance outcomes. Why it is needed: the review workflow must end in a formal, validated decision. Expected output: working decision service with transition validation and persistence. Scope: approve, reject, conditional approve, rationale/condition validation, state updates. Explicit exclusions: decision UI.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given a completed review checklist exists and a valid decision is submitted, when the service processes it, then the review item transitions to the correct decision state. | [ReqID: R20] Given the decision is invalid or the item is not ready for decision, when processing is attempted, then the service blocks the action and returns the correct error.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-037 AC1 and AC2. Downstream prerequisite for status-history story.

Title: Do implement reviewer decision UI and status feedback
Description: Action to be performed: build the reviewer interface for approval, rejection, and conditional approval with proper feedback. Why it is needed: reviewers need a usable decision workflow that exposes required rationale or conditions. Expected output: working decision form and status feedback. Scope: decision UI, rationale fields, conditional fields, save feedback. Explicit exclusions: analytics.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given a valid decision and required fields are entered, when the reviewer saves the decision, then the UI shows the updated decision state. | [ReqID: R20] Given required rationale or condition data is missing, when save is attempted, then the UI blocks the action and highlights the issue.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-037 AC1 and AC2. Requires stable decision contract from PBI-070.

Title: Do implement decision access control, audit logging, and invalid-state prevention
Description: Action to be performed: add authorization, audit trails, and invalid-state prevention for governance decisions. Why it is needed: decision capture is sensitive and must be traceable and protected. Expected output: protected decision path with audit records and state guards. Scope: reviewer permission checks, decision-event logs, invalid-state blocking. Explicit exclusions: reporting.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given an unauthorized user attempts to record a decision, when access is evaluated, then the action is denied. | [ReqID: R20] Given a decision is attempted from an invalid workflow state, when the system checks it, then the action is blocked and the event is logged as appropriate.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-037 AC1 and AC2. Evidence should include invalid-state protection.

Title: Do execute decision-flow validation, documentation updates, and demo evidence
Description: Action to be performed: validate approve, reject, and conditional-approve flows end to end and prepare evidence. Why it is needed: the story is not Done until all decision variants are proven and documented. Expected output: completed tests, updated docs, and demo material. Scope: multi-path functional tests, regression checks, docs, screenshots, demo evidence. Explicit exclusions: new feature logic.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given decision implementation is complete, when end-to-end validation runs, then approve, reject, and conditional-approve flows behave as expected. | [ReqID: R20] Given the story is reviewed, when evidence is attached, then test results, screenshots, and updated documentation are available.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-037 AC1 and AC2 plus DoD. Should include evidence for all three decision outcomes.

Title: Do define status-history query model and review progression data contract
Description: Action to be performed: define how current status and review history will be queried and presented. Why it is needed: the view story depends on stable read models and history mapping rules. Expected output: reviewed query contract and progression model. Scope: current-status derivation, action history model, response shape. Explicit exclusions: UI rendering.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given review progression data is analyzed, when the query model is finalized, then the current-status and prior-action structures are explicitly defined. | [ReqID: R20] Given incomplete or intermediate histories exist, when mapping rules are checked, then the response behavior for those states is documented.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-038 AC1 and AC2. Shared contract for API and UI work.

Title: Do implement status-history API and current-status query service
Description: Action to be performed: build backend read logic for current approval status and prior review history. Why it is needed: the coordinator needs a reliable source of workflow transparency. Expected output: working query service returning current state and history. Scope: read endpoint, history retrieval, current-status derivation, intermediate-state support. Explicit exclusions: UI layer.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given a review item exists, when the query service is called, then the current status and prior review actions are returned in the expected structure. | [ReqID: R20] Given no final decision exists yet, when the query runs, then the service still returns the submitted and in-progress states without error.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-038 AC1 and AC2. Depends on real decision data from PBI-070.

Title: Do implement coordinator status-history UI and empty or intermediate-state handling
Description: Action to be performed: build the coordinator view for current status and decision history, including incomplete-history cases. Why it is needed: transparency requires a usable interface, not only an API. Expected output: working status-history screen with safe handling for empty or intermediate states. Scope: current status display, history timeline or list, no-decision-yet state, decision summary. Explicit exclusions: export feature.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given a review item with history exists, when the coordinator opens the screen, then the current status and prior actions are displayed clearly. | [ReqID: R20] Given no final decision exists yet, when the coordinator opens the screen, then the UI shows the available progress states without errors.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-038 AC1 and AC2. Must explicitly handle empty and intermediate histories.

Title: Do implement view permissions, audit read logging, and response hardening for history access
Description: Action to be performed: add permissions, read-audit logging, and safe response handling for status-history access. Why it is needed: governance history is sensitive and must be read only by authorized roles. Expected output: protected read path with logging and safe handling of incomplete data. Scope: permission checks, read-event logs, safe response rules. Explicit exclusions: analytics dashboard.
AcceptanceCriteria: [ReqID: R20] Given an unauthorized user attempts to view review history, when access is evaluated, then the request is denied. | [ReqID: R20] Given an authorized user views the history, when the response is returned, then read access is logged and incomplete histories are handled safely.
Notes: Important: Covers PBI-038 AC1 and AC2. Read logging matters for governance evidence.
